notes:
Live codex-cli 0.144.5 PROBE (META-HARNESS-122, probe 1 / commit-key matrix, permissions_prompt family): does a bare-CR digit selection actually commit an /permissions menu row, or does it need CSI 13 u, and does bursting the digit+submit as ONE pty write vs TWO separate writes matter?

Recorded by test/corpus/tools/probe-codex-permissions.ts --phase permissions --open-shape split, under an isolated CODEX_HOME (auth.json only, no config.toml, so the dialog opens on '1. Ask for approval (current)' every time). All four cells ran in ONE continuous session: the dialog was reopened via /permissions before each cell and backed out+composer-cleared after, so the SAME process/composer state carried across cells (contrast: the update_notice family below, which needed a fresh process per cell because its notice renders only once per boot). The target digit alternates 2/3 (Approve for me / Full Access) across cells specifically so no cell ever re-commits the row already '(current)' — a successful commit is otherwise invisible on screen.

RESULT — ALL FOUR CELLS COMMITTED, including the bare-CR burst:
  * cr-burst-d2    '2\r'              ONE write  -> committed=true ("Permissions updated to Approve for me")
  * cr-split-d3    '3' then '\r'      TWO writes -> committed=true ("Permissions updated to Full Access")
  * csi13u-burst-d2 '2\x1b[13u'       ONE write  -> committed=true ("Permissions updated to Approve for me")
  * csi13u-split-d3 '3' then '\x1b[13u' TWO writes -> committed=true ("Permissions updated to Full Access")

This CONTRADICTS the theory in ready.ts's submitKeyForHarness / conversation.ts's writeMessageAndSubmit docstring that a bare CR from a synthetic PTY writer is not a submit under the kitty keyboard protocol: that theory is about the free-text COMPOSER message-submission path (where a text+Enter burst risks being consumed as a paste with the Enter rendered as a literal newline). Menu-row selection is a different input path — parseMenuOptions's existing 'digit + bare CR' (codex.ts:597) already commits reliably here, burst or split, for this family. No encoding change is needed for permissions_prompt specifically; see the update_notice and approval_prompt fixtures alongside this one for whether all three families agree (cross-reference in the parent ticket report).

CONTAINMENT: the isolated home's config.toml ended with approvals_reviewer="user" (the last-committed preset was Full Access, csi13u-split-d3); real ~/.codex/config.toml and ~/.codex/auth.json were both sha256-identical before and after (see containment block) — the writes landed only in the isolated CODEX_HOME. stdin.log records every keystroke escape-encoded and IS the byte pin for this session.
